demos.title: Performance Optimization — chalk library

key_frames.caption: chalk documentation — 5th most downloaded npm package
Claude creates todo list and runs benchmarks
CPU profiling to identify slow operations

walkthrough_steps: Instructor introduces chalk — a JS package for colored terminal text, 5th most downloaded in npm (429M/week)
Asks Claude Code to find and optimize performance issues in chalk
Claude creates a todo list to track progress
Runs benchmarks to identify worst performing cases
Writes a file to zoom in on one particular case
Uses CPU profiler to understand why that case is slow
Implements improvements — achieves 3.9x throughput improvement

demos.title: Data Analysis — CSV churn analysis in Jupyter notebook

key_frames.caption: CSV dataset of video streaming platform users
Claude runs analysis in Jupyter notebook
Claude executes cells and views results to customize analysis

walkthrough_steps: Instructor provides CSV file with video streaming platform user data
Asks Claude to do general analysis and identify causes of churn in a Jupyter notebook
Claude writes code into notebook cells, executes them, and views results
Claude customizes successive cells based on previous execution results
Demonstrates Claude's ability to not just write code but also execute and iterate

demos.title: UI Styling with Playwright MCP — extending Claude Code

key_frames.caption: UI generation app — chat interface and header need styling
Claude given access to Playwright MCP browser tools
Claude opens browser, navigates to app, takes screenshot

walkthrough_steps: Instructor shows a UI generation app — chat interface and header look unstyled
Gives Claude Code access to Playwright MCP server (new browser control tools)
Asks Claude to improve styling and use the browser to verify
Claude opens browser, navigates to app, takes screenshot of current state
Updates styling, takes another screenshot, iterates on design
Result: a polished, professional-looking interface

demos.title: GitHub PR Review — catching PII exposure in Terraform infrastructure

key_frames.caption: Claude Code GitHub Actions integration overview
AWS infrastructure diagram — DynamoDB, Lambda, S3 bucket
One-line code change adds user email to shared S3 bucket
Pull request with the email addition change
Claude Code automated review catches PII exposure to external partner

walkthrough_steps: Explains Claude Code can run in GitHub Actions — triggered by PRs or issue mentions
Sets up scenario: AWS infrastructure with DynamoDB → Lambda → S3 bucket
S3 bucket shared with internal AND external marketing team
Months later, internal team requests adding user email to bucket export
Developer adds one line to Lambda function — forgets bucket is shared externally
This exposes PII (email) to external partner — a serious security issue
Claude Code automated PR review catches this: identifies data flow and PII exposure
Review shows exact steps of data flow and explains the external partner risk
